name: Bizcochos
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Sweet Sherry, such as Harvey's Bristol Cream
recipeInstructions:
These are a traditional Mexican cookie and must be made with lard to have the right consistency. So, eat a lot of oat bran the next day...don't forget one of the best cookies you will ever, ever eat.
1. Position a rack in the upper and lower thirds of the oven and preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. In a mixer, whip the lard until it is light and creamy. Beat in the sugar, sherry, orange juice, egg yolk, and flour until well combined.
3. Roll the dough out between layers of waxed paper to a thickness of 1/2 inch. Cut out cookies in whatever shapes you wish and transfer them to ungreased baking sheets.
4. Bake for 12-15 minutes, exchanging the position of the sheets on the racks from top to bottom and front to back at the halfway point, until the cookies are crisp and lightly colored.
5. For the coating, mix the sugar, cinnamon, and cloves in a wide shallow dish (like a pie plate).
6. Carefully dredge the warm cookies in the spiced sugar mixture.
7. Cool completely on a rack. Store at room temperature in airtight tins.
